title: End of Evangelion spoilers abound
wikitext:
[]: End of Evangelion - spoilers abound             Quote Post by Guybrush » Wed Jan 26, 2011 12:53 am Here is my understanding of the events at the end of End of Evangelion: -Instrumentality succeeds, but only partially. Instead of becoming a god-like unified being, humanity is unified but confined to a goo of LCL filling the seas of Earth. This is because Shinji chose it to be so. -The goo contains all persons who have ever lived, except for Yui, who remains within Eva 01. I am not sure if Gendo is included in the LCL goo or not, since he may have been deliberately excluded by Rei. It is also unclear whether any of the 17 Angels/Apostles or Adam or Lilith are included in the goo. Judging by the apparent death of Lilith and Kaworu/Tabris, I am guessing only humans are included in the LCL goo. -Anyone within the LCL goo has the choice to leave the goo and become a separate individual human, as Shinji chose to do. They will be immortal, but can never return to the LCL goo. (Immortal because the Rei/Lilith thing says while Shinji is returning to human form "don't worry, all living things have the ability to be resurrected, and the will to go on living.") -Asuka chooses to leave the goo in order to be with Shinji. I assume she does this because she loves him (even though she finds his weakness 'disgusting' she still loves the whole person that is Shinji). -Because humanity did not become a unified god-like being, they are stuck on Earth in their goo and will all die once the Sun burns out. Once this occurs, Yui in Eva 01 will be the only evidence that humanity ever existed. Had Shinji permitted Instrumentality to go forward fully, humanity would have merged with Adam and Lilith and become a being with equivalent power to the Ancestral Race that created Adam and Lilith. They would not have been limited to that solar system or even to that galaxy. So - I am sure some of this must be wrong - anyone have any corrections?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Jan 26, 2011 12:57 am No immortality. Asuka's reasons for leaving are unknown. Last point entirely unknown. Also, you don't need to mark spoilers for stuff related to the original series. We've all seen it here. No biggie though.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jan 26, 2011 2:32 am Unsurprisingly, a popular topic for discussion : a sample of related threads being -- http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=10063 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=9468 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=9001 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=8496 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=7776 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3973 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=1457 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=1558 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=1232 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=459 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=2458 Anno described the whole thing thus : Hideaki Anno wrote: "It's a work that mirrors the self of each and every person that watches it. That's because [the show contains] an excessive amount of information, and the projections of the viewers simply return to them. For each person, the appeal [of the show] is also different."

[]: Re: End of Evangelion - spoilers abound             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Jan 26, 2011 6:53 am Guybrush wrote: -Asuka chooses to leave the goo in order to be with Shinji. I assume she does this because she loves him (even though she finds his weakness 'disgusting' she still loves the whole person that is Shinji).

[]: Re: End of Evangelion - spoilers abound             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 26, 2011 7:23 am Guybrush wrote: Here is my understanding of the events at the end of End of Evangelion: -Instrumentality succeeds, but only partially. Instead of becoming a god-like unified being, humanity is unified but confined to a goo of LCL filling the seas of Earth. This is because Shinji chose it to be so.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Wed Jan 26, 2011 5:32 pm Last thing we need is another "What the hell happened in EoE" thread. Can someone merge this with one of the other topics Mr. Tines provided?
